claim_boundary: This extension bundle is a truthful 32k/64k/131k served-shape replay lane derived from larger source workloads. It is not a native 500k+/1M+ InferenceX served-lane claim.

runtime_boundary.notes: Hermes/OpenShell/cloud-code-like synthetic generation stays external/offline. InferenceX consumes generated files only and does not run a live synthetic runtime.

content_blocks.text: We are resuming the infra migration memo from last week. Keep this thread text-only. The last checkpoint said we had three blockers: H100 memory pressure, resume latency spikes, and handoff confusion between regions. Start by restating the current state from memory before we revise it.

We are resuming the infra migration memo from last week. Keep this thread text-only. The last checkpoint said we had three blockers: H100 memory pressure, resume latency spikes, and handoff confusion between regions. Start by restating the current state from memory before we revise it.

From prior turns: (1) H100 clusters were stable at lc3 but degraded when concurrent resume windows stacked; (2) H200 improved headroom but not enough to absorb every ultra-long continuation; (3) B200 solved headroom but introduced higher operational overhead. We also agreed that resume spikes correlated with compacted-context quality, not only raw KV capacity.
Good. Add the new constraint: legal requires data-local processing for the EU lane, so we cannot centralize all resume traffic in US-East anymore. How does that change the draft plan?
